methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Floodbase, a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Bessie Schwarz and Dr. Beth Tellman, has raised $17M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable set spanning 8 private companies and 1 public company in the Climate & Energy sector, including peers such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $34M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-stage, same-sector comparables with valuations of $110M, $150M, and $200M; the median of $150M reflects the broader market positioning for frontier-tech companies at this stage.

The triangulated range is $29M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $92M across both methods. The wide spread reflects the material uncertainty inherent in early-stage climate tech valuation—peer comparables suggest substantially higher valuations than stage-progression alone, indicating market premium for the sector. Key uncertainties include product maturity relative to peers, revenue traction, and the sustainability of elevated Climate & Energy valuations in the current environment. A midpoint of $60M–$120M appears most defensible pending customer and unit economics detail.
